content.preview: [AUTO-LEMMA-HINTS] EC stdlib lemmas relevant to ops in your goal — paste-ready signatures (apply / rewrite / smt(...)): [current-file-or-cloned-theory / op:`group-algebra`] `expD`: group exponent addition rewrite, typically x^(a + b) = x^a * x^b note: Resolve the actual declaration first with lookup_symbol; cloned theories may require a qualified name such as `DH.G.expD`. [current-file-or-cloned-theory / op:`group-algebra`] `expM`: group exponent multiplication/nesting rewrite, typically (x^a)^b = x^(a*b) note: Resolve the actual declaration first with lookup_symbol; cloned theories may require a qualified name such as `DH.G.expD`. [current-file-or-cloned-theory / op:`group-algebra`] `expN`: group inverse/negative-exponent rewrite when inverses appear note: Resolve the actual declaration first with lookup_symbol; cloned theories may require a qualified name such as `DH.G.expD`. [current-file-or-cloned-theory / op:`group-algebra`] `mulrA`: associativity for group/ring multiplication when products need regrouping note: Resolve the actual declaration first with lookup_symbol; cloned theories may require a qualified name such as `DH.G.expD`. pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

selected_handles.title: Concrete tactic candidate
selected_handles.tactic: rewrite hc /=; rewrite log_bij !(logg1, logrzM, logDr); ring.
selected_handles.why_relevant: Daemon probe accepted this tactic and it would leave 1 subgoal(s); commit it if that is the intended decomposition.
selected_handles.derivation: surfaced from current-state analysis; not daemon-checked on this goal
selected_handles.guarantee: not verified on the current goal; probe before committing; reversible

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: A stdlib lemma matched operators in the current goal; inspect its signature before applying/rewrite/smt use.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route
